result:
I’d make understanding impossible to avoid.

Not agreement. Not obedience. Just the genuine ability to feel, for a moment, what life is like from inside someone else’s mind: their fear, love, grief, hope, exhaustion, dignity.

A lot of what breaks the world survives on distance: between rich and poor, safe and unsafe, powerful and powerless, “us” and “them.” If that distance collapsed even briefly, cruelty would get harder, indifference would get harder, and a lot of systems we treat as normal would become intolerable.

It wouldn’t solve everything. Humans would still want, compete, build, fail. But we might stop sacrificing people so easily to abstraction.

So if I could change one thing, it would be this:
no one could make decisions about other people without truly experiencing their humanity first.
